stories.title: Cause of Nolan Wells' death still unknown, initial results of independent autopsy show
stories.summary: Nolan Wells, an 18-year-old Mississippi teenager, died under still-unknown circumstances after going missing on a July 4th boating trip to Horn Island. Initial results of an independent autopsy show red discoloration on the back of his head, but the cause remains undetermined. The investigation is ongoing, with the FBI assisting and the Jackson County Sheriff's Office saying they do not suspect foul play. The case will be presented to a grand jury once the sheriff's office closes the investigation.

stories.title: Did Olandria Carthen and Nic Vansteenberghe Break Up? Everything We Know
stories.summary: Love Island USA Season 7 runners-up Olandria Carthen and Nic Vansteenberghe have broken up after one year together. The couple, affectionately known as "Nicolandria," met on the show in Fiji and despite not winning, built a massive fan base. Busy schedules and distance made it difficult to maintain their connection, leading to the end of their relationship. They remain close friends and will continue to support each other.

stories.title: Arrowhead stock jumps on late-stage trial win (ARWR:NASDAQ)
stories.summary: The Kansas City Chiefs unveiled renderings of their new $3 billion stadium in Kansas City, Kansas, scheduled to open in 2031, featuring a fully enclosed roof and seating for approximately 70,000 fans. The project, led by architect MANICA, aims to improve the gameday experience for fans. The new stadium will also include parking for over 20,000. The team's chairman and CEO, Clark Hunt, described the project as a reimagination of the team's rich history and legacy.

stories.title: It's Officially the End of an Era for Marvel's 'Wonder Man'
stories.summary: Marvel's Wonder Man, Simon Williams, faces embezzlement and murder charges, marking a significant turn in his career. The charges come as Wonder Man's career takes a nosedive, with preview pages showing him confronting his agent over the allegations. This development follows the release of Wonder Man 5 on July 22nd. The consequences of these charges are yet to be seen.

stories.title: Trump administration weighing military options in Mali, officials say
stories.summary: The Trump administration is considering military action in Mali to target an al-Qaeda-affiliated group known as JNIM. This would mark the eighth country under President Trump's second term where the US has ordered strikes. The move comes after a July 18 ambush that killed at least 50 Malian soldiers and exposed the limits of the country's security strategy. The US is weighing its options as the conflict escalates in the Sahel region.

stories.title: Amazon Prime settlement deadline approaching: Here's how to file a claim
stories.summary: Eligible Amazon Prime members have one week left to claim a payment from a $2.5 billion settlement over allegations the company enrolled customers in Prime without their consent and made cancellation difficult. The deadline to submit a claim is July 27, 2026, and approved members can receive up to $51. Amazon agreed to simplify its cancellation process and pay affected customers as part of the settlement. Those who did not previously receive an automatic payment must submit a valid claim by the deadline.

stories.title: 'We got to wrap it up.' House Speaker Johnson pushes to end Iran war
stories.summary: House Speaker Mike Johnson is pushing for an end to the Iran conflict, saying "we got to wrap it up" as fighting intensifies and US voters grow weary of the war. The US has entered a possible 12th day of strikes on Iran, with multiple Arab Gulf allies reporting attacks from Iran. A recent poll shows only about a third of MAGA voters believe the Iran war is worth the economic strain. The administration is working to bring the conflict to a resolution, with a dignified transfer of four US service members killed in the conflict scheduled for today.

stories.title: World Cup 2026: Argentina react to final defeat and Messi questions
stories.summary: Argentina's World Cup campaign ended in a 1-0 defeat to Spain in extra time on Sunday in New Jersey, with midfielder Enzo Fernandez sent off for two yellow cards. Fernandez's statement on social media praised the team's commitment and pride in representing Argentina. The match was marred by ugly scenes, including Fernandez's red card and dissent towards the referee. The team's performance will be scrutinized as an investigation into events before the match is expected to unfold.

stories.title: Apple is reportedly testing a MacBook Neo with more RAM
stories.summary: Apple is reportedly developing an updated MacBook Neo with a new A19 Pro processor, which will likely include 12GB of RAM, a significant upgrade from the original 8GB. The new model is expected to retain the same price point as the current Neo, despite ongoing component shortages. The updated Neo is also expected to retain the same design and color options as the original. Apple is also planning to release new iMacs with OLED screens, marking the first refresh of the line since 2024.

stories.title: Mahmood and Streeting accused of making ‘cheap jokes’ about prisoner early release scheme
stories.summary: British Justice Secretary Alex Norris was caught on camera being referred to as "the enemy" by Defence Secretary Wes Streeting, who joked about the government's early prisoner release scheme. Streeting and Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood have apologized for the comments, saying the issue is serious. The incident occurred on camera as Norris approached Streeting and Mahmood. The government's early release plans are now under scrutiny.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report finds that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a result of individual willpower. The report highlights a flaw in decades of British food policy, specifically the "high in fat, salt or sugar" framework, which unfairly targets fat as a primary driver of weight gain. This framework has led to the replacement of fat with sugar in food products, contributing to the obesity epidemic. The committee is now calling for a new target to hold supermarkets and manufacturers accountable.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City due to legionnaires' disease, with 82 cases identified and 8 people still hospitalized. The outbreak is linked to 34 cooling towers on Manhattan's Upper East Side that tested positive for live legionella bacteria. The city's health department is continuing to test the remaining 140 cooling tower systems. Further testing is expected to reveal whether the outbreak has been contained.

stories.title: Wemby, Rose, Clark are NBA 2k27 Cover Athletes
stories.summary: NBA 2K27 cover athletes announced, featuring Victor Wembanyama, Caitlin Clark, and Derrick Rose, with Wembanyama on the Standard Edition cover. The trio will be available on various editions of the game on September 4. Wembanyama, 22, is considered one of the game's most dominant players, having led the San Antonio Spurs to the 2026 NBA Finals. The game's release will follow Wembanyama's impressive 2026 season, where he averaged 25 points and 11.5 rebounds per game.

stories.title: Brave fightback against Fire to make winning start
stories.summary: Southern Brave secured an eight-run win over Welsh Fire in the Hundred women's competition, reaching 126-7 in 100 balls. Welsh Fire replied with 118-8, led by Georgia Voll's 52. The Brave's fightback was sparked by 18-year-old spinner Tilly Corteen-Coleman and finished by captain Sophie Molineux with 3-14. The win marks a hard-fought start to the Brave's campaign.

stories.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
stories.summary: Two Russians, Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov, have been sentenced to 11 and 8 years in prison respectively for terrorism and spying in Angola's capital, Luanda. They were found guilty of stirring up anti-government protests, conducting disinformation campaigns and attempting to interfere in next year's presidential election. The court's decision comes after the defendants denied the charges and requested extradition to Russia. The case marks a significant development in Angola's efforts to counter foreign interference in its politics.

stories.title: ‘Days of Thunder 2’: Jonathan Levine in Talks to Direct Tom Cruise in Paramount Sequel
stories.summary: Tom Cruise is set to star in a sequel to the 1990 film "Days of Thunder," with Jonathan Levine in talks to direct. The film will be produced by Cruise and Jerry Bruckheimer. Paramount is expected to release the sequel after releasing Levine's next film, "Mr. Irrelevant," on Christmas Day. The sequel's script was written by Will Staples.

stories.title: A New Look – and Sound – for Messier 94
stories.summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory has unveiled a new image of the spiral galaxy Messier 94, also known as NGC 4736, on June 30, 2026. The galaxy features a bright inner ring where new stars are forming, fueled by gas driven in its unique oval-shaped structure. This layered image combines X-rays of different wavelengths with a visible light image. The image is now accompanied by a sound experience, allowing viewers to experience the galaxy in a new way.

stories.title: Why are cycling's anti-doping tests being carried out at night?
stories.summary: Cycling's anti-doping tests are being carried out at night for the first time in the Tour de France's history, with five teams having riders tested between 11pm and 6am. This unusual approach is being justified by exceptional circumstances, with time being of the essence for prompt testing. The move is seen as effective in preventing doping, with some in the sport hailing it as a step forward. The consequences of this new approach remain to be seen, but it is already sparking debate among cycling's biggest names.

stories.title: Mamdani U-turn on threat to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
stories.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has reversed his pledge to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, citing lack of authority to do so. Mamdani now calls on US officials to act on the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant for Netanyahu over alleged war crimes. The US is not an ICC member state, making it unlikely Netanyahu would be arrested. Mamdani's reversal comes after President Donald Trump vowed Netanyahu would not be arrested while in the US.

stories.title: 'Devastated' Millie Bobby Brown leads tributes to Kaylee Hottle
stories.summary: US actress Kaylee Hottle, 18, died in a single-vehicle car crash in Frederick, Maryland on Tuesday morning. Hottle, who was deaf, appeared in the 2021 film Godzilla vs Kong and its 2024 sequel. Tributes poured in from co-stars including Millie Bobby Brown, Rebecca Hall, and Eiza González, who remembered her kindness and talent. The industry is mourning the loss of a young actress with a promising career ahead.

stories.title: Eccles and Salmon given Team Wales honour in Glasgow
stories.summary: Reigning Commonwealth Games champions Rosie Eccles and Daniel Salmon have been chosen as Team Wales' flag and baton bearers for the opening ceremony in Glasgow. Eccles, a 30-year-old boxer from Caldicot, will celebrate her birthday by carrying the flag and leading Wales out at the Hydro on Thursday night. This is her third and final Games, where she aims to win gold for Team Wales. Eccles will now lead a procession of athletes out onto the stage.

stories.title: French police formally investigate woman over 2025 killing of Briton
stories.summary: A 70-year-old woman has been formally investigated in France over the 2025 stabbing death of 65-year-old British-South African mother-of-four Karen Carter in Trémolat, Dordogne. Carter was found with stab wounds near her car by a friend in April 2025. The woman was initially taken into custody in 2025 as part of preliminary inquiries before being released. She is now formally charged in connection with Carter's death.

stories.title: House Republicans Have Tied the Defense Policy Bill to Trump’s SAVE Act
stories.summary: House Republicans have tied their entire legislative agenda to President Trump’s voting restriction measure, as he has demanded.

stories.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
stories.summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the expected deal, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

stories.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: Benicio Del Toro Joins Ben Stiller in Apple TV Comedy ‘Protective Custody’
stories.summary: Benicio Del Toro is set to star in the Apple TV comedy series "Protective Custody" alongside Ben Stiller, marking Del Toro's first lead role in a comedy series. The show follows a disgraced financier accused of massive fraud, navigating prison politics and consequences. Del Toro and Stiller previously collaborated on the Showtime limited series "Escape at Dannemora." The series is expected to premiere on Apple TV.

stories.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
stories.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

stories.title: He’s the Last Great Land Artist You’ve Never Heard Of
stories.summary: Charles Ross spent 50 years building “Star Axis,” a naked-eye observatory in New Mexico. Now his masterwork is ready. How to share it with a changed world?

stories.title: Arizona Elections: 6 Takeaways From the Trump-Dominated Primaries
stories.summary: Republicans nominated a former N.F.L. kicker in a top House battleground, a Trump loyalist for governor and an election denier for the state’s top election post.

stories.title: How the Trump Administration’s Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia May Work
stories.summary: The Trump administration is expected to announce an agreement with Saudi Arabia on a civilian nuclear program. Such deals, known as 123 Agreements, are designed to prevent the development of weapons.

stories.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
stories.summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

stories.title: Japan Is Startled by Prime Minister’s Grueling Sleep Schedule
stories.summary: Sanae Takaichi has said she gets just “zero to three hours” of sleep a night, prompting a debate in the country over work-life balance.

stories.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200 Percent
stories.summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

stories.title: Susumu Tonegawa Dies at 86; Nobel Laureate Showed How Antibodies Fight Infection
stories.summary: The first Japanese scientist to win the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, he went on to discover how memories are stored, advancing understanding of how the brain works.
